FP64 Cholesky chain + H symmetrization in GPTQ: factor H in double precision (symmetrized first) to eliminate fp32 round-off amplification in the upper-triangular error-propagation step, critical at the narrow damping=1.01 operating point.

Fisher-weighted GPTQ Hessian: weight per-token x⊗x by ||∂L/∂y_t||² (output-side gradient norm) on attn/mlp linears, mean-normalized per-batch so total H mass matches baseline.

Per-pass loop offset: single learnable [model_dim] vector (zero-init), modulated by hard-coded linear-ramp pass scalar (0, 0.5, 1.0) and added to residual at entry to each call of looped blocks 3-5; breaks translational symmetry of the 3 identical recurrence passes via PR1518-style additive feature-level offset, byte-cheap (one [512] param vs num_loops+1 vectors).
Per-group Muon mm_blend SHAPE split: attn (g0) uses sqrt-with-floor (max(lr_scale**0.5, 0.25)), mlp (g1) keeps linear-with-floor (max(lr_scale, 0.25)); shared floor=0.25 and cool target=0.93.

Mean-preserving token-id-stratified train CE: w=0.9+0.2*(target_id/8191) probes Gap 5 rare-token upweight at 2x magnitude vs exp_518's 0.10 ramp.

Replace TTT-only z-loss L2 form (1e-5*logsumexp^2.mean) with L4 form (6e-8*logsumexp^4.mean) — coef tuned to match L2's typical gradient magnitude at logsumexp~9; L4 penalizes outlier high-partition positions more aggressively than L2 while leaving low-logsumexp positions nearly untouched. Untested neighbor on z-loss-norm axis (L1 exp_771 discard, L2 exp_596 keep, variance exp_675 size_blocked); byte-neutral 1-char swap.

grad_clip_norm 0.3→0.28 — untested gentle DOWN midpoint between current 0.3 and exp_525's size_blocked-at-improvement 0.25, consistent with the axis's DOWN-favoring asymmetry (0.4 UP discard +0.000340 vs 0.2 DOWN discard +0.000124, 0.25 DOWN size_blocked at bpb 1.074731 = trial-time improvement).

With exp_596 z-loss softening inner CE gradients and Nesterov+mu=0.9 amplifying outliers, a 20% tighter cap should stabilize early-chunk trajectories without the over-constraining that hurt prior 0.5 probes.
Narrow AWQ-style per-row scale-grid refinement (alpha in {0.95, 1.0, 1.05}) before GPTQ inner loop, picking alpha minimizing H-diag-weighted RTN error per row.

Asymmetric LayerScale init for parallel-residual blocks (7-10): mlp_scale starts at 0.5 instead of 1.0; attn_scale stays 1.0. Breaks the implicit equal-magnitude prior between parallel attn/mlp pathways and biases optimizer to attn-dominant features early; mlp grows during training.

Mean-preserving per-epoch linear LR decay within each TTT chunk (1.25x→0.75x of cos_lr across the 4 inner epochs): freshest-data epoch 0 gets the largest step, later epochs polish with smaller steps as gradient signal becomes increasingly correlated by re-traversing the same chunk; total LR budget per chunk is preserved.

Schedule optimizer_tok (AdamW on tied tok_emb) beta2 from 0.95 (peak-LR) up to 0.99 (deepest tail) lockstep with (1-lr_scale): longer second-moment EMA window in warmdown gives more stable preconditioner for rare-token rows whose gradients are sparse and small.
Threshold-gated AWQ-style per-row scale refinement: keep grid alpha in {0.95,1.0,1.05} but only swap from alpha=1.0 if alt H-diag-weighted MSE less than 0.95x baseline.
SCALAR_LR 0.02 → 0.018 (gentle DOWN -10%, untested midpoint between current and exp_204's failed -25% probe at depth 10).

Milder asymmetric init for parallel-residual blocks (7-10): mlp_scale=0.8 (vs 1.0 baseline, attn=1.0). Same Z2-symmetry-breaking mechanism as exp_788's mlp_scale=0.5 (which gave val_bpb=1.072608, Δ≈-0.0006 but size_blocked at +5KB), at smaller magnitude to keep trained weights closer to baseline GPTQ entropy and fit under 16MB cap.

Per-group LR floor on optimizer_tok only at 5% of base_lr — keeps tied tok_emb's rare-token rows still learning through deep warmdown when Muon/scalar/head groups have effectively stopped updating; orthogonal to global MIN_LR axis (exp_660 discard, hurt all groups uniformly) and Muon-only floor (exp_680 discard).

Stack Gradient Centralization (zero-mean per-row gradient on 2D+ ttt_params before clip+step) onto current best exp_750 — exp_750 forked from exp_596 (z-loss) and added the opt mm_blend tweak, but never inherited GC from exp_592/exp_746; the GC+z-loss combination at exp_746 was kept (Δ=-0.000005 over z-loss alone), so applying the same row-mean grad correction on top of the strictly-stronger 750 stack is the natural untried recombination.

Temperature-scale TTT inner CE: F.cross_entropy(_lf/1.1,...) — softens TTT per-chunk targets to T=1.1, reducing 21-epoch chunk overfitting, mechanism-distinct from all 18 prior z-loss-form variants (L1/L2/L4, hinge, var, anchor, max, decay, gate, scale all tested) which all modify the regularizer term, not the CE itself.

Pair brotli lgwin=22→24 (untried alone on current parent; estimated 150–500KB savings on the ~16MB compressed payload via 4× larger LZ77 window) with the threshold-gated AWQ-3 scale grid (α∈{0.95,1.0,1.05}, only swap if H-diag-weighted MSE drops >5%) — exp_791 measured the gated-grid at val_bpb=1.071846 size_blocked +285KB on this same exp_750 parent, so lgwin=24 is exactly the size-saver needed to land that quality move under the cap.
Stride+1 packing in _reset_shard: stride from seq_len (2048) to seq_len+1 (2049). Decorrelates seq-pos and file-pos modular coupling.

Forward-time per-row RMS-equalized tied-readout: F.linear(x, F.rms_norm(tok_emb.weight, (D,)) * tied_embed_init_std) so every token's classifier row has identical L2=std*sqrt(D), decoupling logit-magnitude from row-magnitude drift while keeping raw tok_emb at the lookup path. Parameter-free, init-logit-magnitude-preserving, untried in tok history.

Loop-iteration-aligned U-Net split: shift num_enc 8->9 (len(all_indices)//2 + 1) so encoder ends at block-5-iter1 boundary and decoder starts at block-3-iter2; LIFO skip pairing now feeds prior-iter-end into next-iter-start (block-5-iter1 -> block-3-iter2) and same-block recurrent feedback (block-4-iter1 -> block-4-iter2). Block-0 output drops from skips; num_skip_weights stays at 8. Zero new params, byte-neutral.

Per-group Muon matrix_lr split: introduce matrix_lr_mlp=0.023 for MLP Muon group while attn group keeps matrix_lr=0.022 (per-group base_lr). Mirrors the kept attn/mlp WD asymmetry (exp_164/exp_336) on the LR axis.
TTT input-token dropout p=0.03 mask-to-0 before forward_logits in eval_val_ttt train phase: token-level augmentation regularizes inner SGD against per-chunk overfitting.

Recurrence-aware per-block-group TTT LR: divide TTT lr by sqrt(num_loops+1)=sqrt(3) on looped blocks 3-5 only (the blocks invoked 3x per forward), leaving other blocks at full lr. Mirrors exp_245's kept ln_scale variance-balancing on these same blocks.

Mismatch-gated mean-preserving TTT z-loss: replace `1e-5*lse².mean()` with `1e-5*sum(lse²·1[ŷ≠y])/sum(1[ŷ≠y])` on the TTT inner CE — penalize log-partition magnitude only on positions where argmax prediction is wrong, leaving already-correct positions with unconstrained logit-magnitude freedom for sharpening. Mechanism-distinct from all 11 prior TTT z-loss probes (L1/variance/anchor=log(V)/hinge/tail-PW/amax/coef-halve/coef-double/byte-weight/PW-CE/label-smoothing).

Halve gradient on tied tok_emb classifier path (W_cls = 0.5*W + 0.5*W.detach()): lookup path retains full gradient, output classifier contributes 0.5x backward grad. Forward identity, zero new params. Tests whether dual-pull tied tok_emb benefits from input-lookup-dominant gradient flow.

Enable amsgrad=True on optimizer_tok (tied tok_emb AdamW) only — caps v_t monotonically non-decreasing to fix rare-row Adam-divergence where beta2=0.95 lets v_r decay between row hits causing oversized updates; untouched axis across 800+ trials.
Replace per-row GC with per-column GC on TTT SGD: attacks per-input-feature bias mode. exp_762 doubly-centered failed; pure column-only untested vs kept exp_592 row-only.

Swap MLP activation silu²→gelu² (Primer-style x²-family kept, but use GELU's sharper shoulder vs SiLU): byte-neutral 4-char swap, sparser hidden activations entering int6-quantized proj, untried in this swarm (only silu²/relu² endpoints have been tested).

Predecessor-special CE mask in train_loader: y[x<3]=-100 drops CE on positions where input token is UNK/BOS/EOS, removing gradient noise from document-boundary-conditioned predictions. Mechanism-distinct from exp_679 (target-side mask, discarded +0.0017).
Cool target 0.93→0.94 on Muon momentum mm_blend (single-literal byte-neutral): probe untested UP midpoint between exp_750's kept 0.93 and exp_251's old-baseline-discarded 0.95.

Hard-mask TTT z-loss to last-128 positions to match CE scope: replace logsumexp.mean() over all 2048 positions with logsumexp.mean() over only the last-128 logits (same window where CE is masked). Eliminates z-loss gradient on positions 0..1919 that have no CE signal during TTT; aligns regularization with the actual TTT objective. Add weight_decay=5e-4 to eval-TTT SGD (the only WD=0 optimizer in the pipeline) — clean retry of exp_649 crash (which failed at unrelated dataloader L371) at half strength.

H-magnitude-proportional GPTQ damping: replace uniform H.diag.mul_(1.01) with H.diag.mul_(1 + 0.01 * H_ii / mean(H_ii)) so damping factor scales linearly with column importance — high-H 'sink' columns get up to ~1.05x ridge while low-H get ~1.001x, while AVERAGE multiplier stays at the kept 1.01 sweet spot. Continues the additive→multiplicative trajectory (exp_212→exp_280→exp_377) into a second-order H-proportional bias that no prior probe has touched.
Per-group ADAM_EPS split: optimizer_tok (tied tok_emb) eps 1e-8→1e-7 only, optimizer_scalar unchanged at h.adam_eps=1e-8.
Port exp_711 (embed_scale ONLY on residual baseline x0; entry-x to block 0 plain unit-RMS) onto exp_750 best baseline.

Mirror of kept exp_245's recurrence-aware ln_scale damping, applied to parallel-residual blocks: divide blocks 7-10 (parallel=True) ln_scale_factor by sqrt(2) since each parallel call adds 2 streams (attn+mlp) to residual vs 1 stream for sequential blocks. DeepNorm-style depth-balance for stream multiplicity.

Latin-square phase bucket count 8→6 (untested between failed 5 and kept 8): coupon-collector coverage across 13 shards/rank improves at 2.2 shards/bucket vs current 1.6, at the cost of slightly coarser phase granularity (341 vs 256 tokens).

Polar Express per-iteration NS coefficients (PR #1787 / PR #1344): replace fixed (3.4445,-4.775,2.0315) tuple with 5 minimax-optimized (a,b,c) tuples, one per NS iteration at backend_steps=5. Stack a dormant-by-default entropy-floor hinge on the TTT inner CE: 1e-4 * F.relu(1.0 - H(p)).mean() in addition to kept exp_596 z-loss. Activates only when post-update softmax entropy drops below 1 nat (chunk-overfit collapse), zero gradient on well-calibrated positions — mechanism-distinct from all ~20 prior z-loss / confidence-penalty / amax-logit variants on this axis.

Extend exp_491's selective-freeze policy to also exclude tied tok_emb [8192,512]=4M params during eval-TTT: tok_emb is the single largest tensor and accumulates conflicting gradients per TTT step from its dual role (input embedding + tied LM head), so freezing it removes the dominant TTT-update noise source while leaving 4 MLP/attn matrices to adapt.

Recurrence-aware Muon LR: scale main-train Muon base_lr by 1/sqrt(num_loops+1)=0.577 on looped blocks [3..5] only, equalizing per-step weight-space drift against the sqrt(3)x gradient accumulation those physical blocks see (each invoked 3x per forward).

Half-amplitude rescue of exp_818's size_blocked H-magnitude-proportional damping (bpb=1.072053, Δ=-0.000157, +642B over cap): center the per-column damping spread around mean (=1.01 exactly) with HALF the amplitude — H.diag.mul_(1.01 + 0.005·(H_ii - mean)/mean) so high-H gets up to ~1.025x ridge, low-H gets down to ~1.005x, mean precisely preserved. Half the spread → roughly half the Q-code entropy distortion → expected to fit under 16MB while keeping ~half the bpb gain (-0.00008 estimate).

Replace global L2-norm gradient clip (1.0) with per-element value clip (0.1) during TTT SGD: caps outlier gradient elements that pollute the Nesterov momentum buffer over 13 chunks while preserving direction information in well-behaved tensors that global L2-norm shrinks uniformly.

Per-head sub-LN: F.rms_norm(y, (head_dim,)) on attention output between XSA and attn_out_gate, decoupling gate sigmoid regime + proj's int6 quant input range from V-magnitude × ln_scale_factor variance. Retry of exp_640 mechanism with.contiguous() guard for FA3 non-contig output (suspected prior crash cause).

SCALAR_LR 0.02 → 0.019 (gentle -5% DOWN midpoint between current and exp_792's failed -10% size_blocked-at-improvement-bpb-1.071951): retry the strongest recent meta signal at half the step to pass the size gate, on the proven scalar-control-LR DOWN axis (exp_177 ADAM_WD DOWN kept).

Reparameterize embed_scale [D] from linear (init=1) to log-exp (init=0, applied as torch.exp(log_s)): bitwise-identical step 0, but Adam-WD on log_s pulls s→1 (the init), not s→0 as the current linear param does. Multiplicative-update geometry on the per-feature scale.

Length curriculum: mask y positions seq_len//2.. with -100 for first 25% of train wallclock, forcing CE gradient onto positions 0..1023 (short context) early; full long-context training resumes after.
Replace TTT cosine LR decay with linear (1-ci/(N-1)) across chunks — preserves total LR mass but no early plateau and slightly more tail LR.

Recurrence-aware Muon LR (bugfix retry of exp_827): scale main-train Muon base_lr by 1/sqrt(num_loops+1)=0.577 on looped blocks [3..5] only. Previous trial crashed on name parsing — base_model.blocks.named_parameters() returns names without 'blocks.' prefix, so block_idx = name.split('.')[0] not [1]. Fix is one-character.
Gentle focal CE (γ=0.25, mean-preserving) on TTT inner CE only — first focal probe on TTT axis (prior focal exp_054/144/439/498 all targeted main CE). Composes with kept exp_596 z-loss orthogonally.
SGLD-style Gaussian gradient noise injected into TTT inner-loop SGD (alpha=h.ttt_lr*0.01, added pre-AVG so all_reduce synchronizes noise across ranks).

Tight 3-point per-row GPTQ scale grid {0.96, 1.0, 1.04} on top of current best — partial rescue of exp_728 (5-point {.8,.9,1,1.1,1.25} won val_bpb 1.069841 but size_blocked); tight 4 percent grid limits per-row Q histogram divergence so brotli stays under 16MB cap while still capturing tail-asymmetry signal that fixed clip_sigmas misses.

Re-add exp_588's chunk-boundary momentum_buffer halving (mb.mul_(0.5) for ci>0) onto the new exp_750 baseline, banked by trimming the eval_val_ttt docstring + cosine comment for byte-negative net (-155 packed code bytes)

Replace fixed Newton-Schulz coefficients (3.4445,-4.775,2.0315) with 5 per-iteration minimax-optimal Polar Express tuples (ported from PR#1792/PR#1344): first step uses aggressive (8.16,-22.48,15.88) to rapidly reduce error, subsequent steps use gentler corrections — produces a higher-quality Muon polar factor per gradient step.

Add learnable per-feature bias [embedding_dim=512, init=0] after the embed-RMSNorm, completing the affine transform (scale already learned via embed_scale); zero-init preserves step-0 equivalence, costs ~1 KB in artifact (float16 passthrough).
FreqGPTQ: weight GPTQ Hessian by sqrt(token_frequency/mean_freq) so high-frequency tokens (which dominate BPB) get proportionally higher error-correction priority during quantization.
In parallel-residual blocks (7-10), MLP branch reads pre-mix x while attn keeps reading x_in: asymmetric input routing concentrates x0-anchor through attn only.

Stochastic depth with linear schedule (max_p=0.05 at last layer): randomly replace a block's output with its input during training, using a per-block tensor mask (fullgraph-compile safe), forcing ensemble-like subnetwork diversity across the 11 blocks and 3× recurrent loop calls.
Per-group ADAM_EPS split: raise scalar-group AdamW eps 1e-8 to 1e-7 only, leave optimizer_tok at h.adam_eps. Inverse of exp_819 (tok-only raise, hurt).

Add Gradient Centralization to TTT SGD: zero-mean each 2D+ parameter gradient across input dims after DDP all_reduce and before clip_grad_norm. GC won Δ=−0.000300 on exp_538 (exp_592) and Δ=−0.000005 on exp_592 (exp_746), but was never applied to exp_750's branch (previous attempt exp_627 was size_blocked at ~23K packed bytes; current workdir is 19,856 packed bytes with +52B headroom).

Fuse all_reduce loop with per-row Gradient Centralization (g.sub_(g.mean(1,True)) for 2D TTT params) in a single compact loop — equivalent to exp_592's GC that gave Δ=-0.000300 on exp_538, now applied to exp_750 baseline; uses dim==2 shorthand to stay within size budget (+34 packed bytes vs exp_795's larger per-row GC that size_blocked).

Byte-weighted TTT CE (via val_data.base_bytes_lut + has_leading_space correction, normalized to mean=1) aligns TTT adaptation gradient with BPB metric: multi-byte subword tokens get proportionally more loss weight, matching how they contribute more to bits-per-byte evaluation. Composes with kept z-loss (exp_596). Adapted from PR #731's byte-weighted TTT technique.

Replace per-shard _phase_epoch+_phase_order lists with a single global phase counter: all shard resets share one permuted sequence, guaranteeing all 8 Latin-square phases are covered in the first 8 resets (vs. only ~6.6 expected with independent per-shard permutations across 13 shards/rank).

Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine shape: same 0→0 LR range and same total LR integral (0.5), but cosine holds LR ~14% higher at mid-warmdown and descends more smoothly — better EMA trajectory throughout the 72% warmdown phase.

Add output-side per-feature embed_scale_out [512, init=1.0] multiplied onto x just before the tied LM-head F.linear, providing independent feature gating for the decoder side distinct from the input-side embed_scale (exp_267/298 kept). Also removes the harmful embed_bias from exp_841 (eval_budget_overrun).

TIED_EMBED_LR 0.03 → 0.028 (gentle DOWN -6.7%): half-step retry of exp_252's failed -13.3% probe (depth 12, +0.001054) under the post-538/596/750 regime where 4 added regularizers (z-loss, attn_out_gate, scalar-freeze, mm_blend cool) have shifted the dominant 4.2M tied tok_emb's LR optimum DOWN, aligned with the recent meta DOWN-direction theme (exp_177 adam_wd, exp_336 muon_wd_mlp, exp_792 scalar_lr signal).

Split GPTQ clip sigmas by layer type: MLP matrices use 15.0σ (wider, preserves outlier weights) while attention matrices keep current 12.85σ — MLP layers have heavier-tailed weight distributions post-training; uniform 12.85σ clips them too aggressively.

Q-only region-split RMSNorm: normalize Q's RoPE region [...,:rope_dims=16] and NoPE region [...,rope_dims:] independently before RoPE+q_gain, while K keeps joint norm. Pairs structurally with kept exp_475 bifurcated q_gain[H,2] which currently amplifies a jointly-normalized Q where the dominant region's RMS shrinks the other's. K stays joint since q_gain only operates on Q.
Add label_smoothing=0.02 to training CE: reduces overconfidence during training, mild regularization, and may improve GPTQ quantization quality by keeping logit scale better-calibrated.

Cosine-decay TTT SGD momentum over chunks (0.9→0.45, floored at 0.5×ttt_momentum) mirroring existing cosine LR decay — early chunks retain high momentum for smooth gradient direction, late chunks use lower momentum for responsive local adaptation; also removes exp_846's GC which caused +0.002 regression.

Muon WD taper: linearly reduce Muon weight_decay from 1.0× to 0.5× during training fraction 0.70→1.00, so late warmdown adapts at a relaxed WD that lets weights settle at their true optimum rather than being pulled toward zero when gradient signal is already tiny.

Halve TTT chunk size 65536→32768: doubles adaptation granularity (finer local specialization per 32K-token segment), increases proportion of tokens scored from an adapted model (~98%→~99%), and gives a more gradual cosine LR decay across chunks — all at zero extra compute since total training steps are unchanged.

Gentle linear position-weight 0.9→1.1 on training CE loss (model.forward only; TTT and sliding eval bypass to forward_logits unaffected): upweights high-context positions aligned with stride-64 eval where ≥1984 context tokens are scored.

Add learnable per-feature additive bias (embed_bias [512], init=0) after the embedding RMSNorm, completing the LayerNorm affine pair (γ=embed_scale inside norm + β=embed_bias after norm), allowing the model to learn a token-independent baseline activation per feature.

Dynamic batch-size curriculum: half-batch (393K tokens) during the pre-warmdown phase (first 28% of wallclock = 168s), full-batch (786K) during warmdown — aligns batch noise with LR: high-LR exploration phase gets ~2x more gradient steps, warmdown gets precise signal.

Disable Nesterov lookahead on Muon during warmdown (set nesterov=False when lr_scale<1.0): near convergence Nesterov overshoots by adding mu*buf on top of the gradient, while pure heavy-ball is more conservative and settles into flatter minima that quantize better under GPTQ int6.

TIED_EMBED_INIT_STD 0.005 → 0.006 (+20% UP, byte-neutral): genuinely untouched axis across 850+ trials. Sets std of tied tok_emb (4.2M params) which serves both input embedding AND output readout. With WARMUP_STEPS=20 fixed and the modern stack stacking 5 added regularizers (z-loss, attn_out_gate, scalar-freeze, GC, mm_blend cool), init magnitude shapes the first ~50 steps' Adam-warmup before regularizers can act; tied path init logit RMS rises from 0.113 to 0.136, modest discriminative signal.

Frequency-weighted GPTQ Hessian: pre-scan calibration batches to count token frequencies, then up-weight top-100 most-frequent tokens by sqrt(2) in Hessian accumulation (H += (x*w)^T @ (x*w)), biasing GPTQ error minimization toward tokens that dominate average BPB.
k_gain[Hkv,2] init=1.0 mirrors exp_475 q_gain[H,2] on K side

Focal loss (γ=2) for TTT inner objective: replace CE with per-token reweighted loss (_ce * (1-exp(-_ce))^2).mean() so adaptation concentrates on document-specific hard tokens rather than uniformly across easy+hard tokens.

Ramp TTT z-loss coefficient linearly from 2.5e-6 (epoch 0) to 1e-5 (epoch 3) via `1e-5*(_ep+1)/h.ttt_epochs`: epoch 0 gets 4× less constraint allowing max adaptation freedom, while final epoch retains the full 1e-5 stabilization; average regularization drops to 6.25e-6 vs current 10e-6.

Cosine-decay TTT z-loss coefficient (1e-5→0 over chunks): cold early chunks get full logsumexp^2 regularization to stabilize logit magnitude, while late adapted chunks get near-zero z-loss so confident sharp predictions aren't penalized by stale regularization.

Split looped-block attn WD into third Muon group: blocks [loop_start=3..loop_end=5] get muon_wd_loop=0.115 (matching MLP WD) vs base attn 0.095, since these blocks are called 3× per forward and receive proportionally more gradient signal (exp_141 principle, never folded into main lineage).

ResFormer Value Residual Learning (arXiv 2410.17897, ACL 2025): per-layer learnable scalar λ blends current V with V_0 from layer 0 attention; init=0 → step-0 identical to baseline. Threaded through Block/Attention forward via v0 tuple-return.

Universal-Transformer-style per-loop-pass additive bias [num_loops+1=3, model_dim=512] added to the residual stream right before each looped-block (3-5) call under looping_active, giving the shared block params an explicit phase tag for the 3 iterative-refinement passes; init=0 → step-0 baseline.
Halve GPTQ block_size 32→16: 2× more error-propagation flushes per matrix, continuing the proven 128→64→32 monotone-improvement trend; code distribution unchanged so size-safe.

Raise EMA decay from 0.9965 to 0.9975: extends the weight-averaging window from ~286 steps to ~400 steps, capturing more of the near-converged warmdown trajectory for smoother GPTQ int6 quantization fidelity.

Stack GC (proven exp_746 keep) + within-chunk TTT cosine LR warmdown (ep_lr = cos_lr * 0.5*(1+cos(π*ep/ttt_epochs)), ep=0→1.0x, ep=3→0.15x) onto exp_750: symmetric extension of the already-working cross-chunk cosine decay into the per-epoch dimension.

TIED_EMBED_INIT_STD 0.005 to 0.004 (-20% DOWN, byte-neutral): symmetric mirror of exp_863's +20% UP probe (discard +0.000190). DOWN direction genuinely untouched. Tighter tok_emb init drops initial logit RMS 0.113 to 0.090, starting softmax closer to uniform so CE gradient flows more uniformly across rare rows in the first ~50 Adam steps before embed_wd=0.085 takes over. Aligned with meta DOWN-direction theme (exp_177 adam_wd, exp_336 muon_wd_mlp, exp_792 scalar_lr signal).

Self-anchored TTT z-loss: replace 1e-5*logsumexp^2.mean() with 1e-5*(logsumexp - anchor).^2.mean() where anchor=per-chunk initial logsumexp captured on first mini-batch — penalizes drift from starting calibration rather than absolute magnitude, reducing competing gradient pressure on well-calibrated chunks while still stabilizing explosive drift.

Inverted linear position ramp on training CE: w[t]=1.25-0.5*t/(T-1) upweights early/short-context positions (t=0→w=1.25) and downweights late/long-context (t=T-1→w=0.75), mean=1.0. TTT and sliding eval bypass via forward_logits. Direction opposite to discarded exp_859 (0.9→1.1 upweight-late); improves short-context language prior quality.

Decouple embed_scale from x0: apply embed_scale only to working stream x entering block 0, while residual baseline x0 uses plain unit-RMS (no embed_scale). Currently x0=x after the scaled norm, so embed_scale affects BOTH paths. With plain x0, each block's mix[1]*x0 residual injection gets an unbiased token reference, letting mix weights learn unconstrained feature selection without embed_scale double-counting the feature weighting.

Universal-Transformer-style per-loop-pass additive bias [num_loops+1=3, model_dim=512] added to the residual stream right before each looped-block (3-5) call, giving the shared block params an explicit phase tag for the 3 iterative-refinement passes; init=0 → step-0 baseline; DDP-safe via zero-coeff anchor pulling all rows into the autograd graph.

TTT MLP-only adaptation: freeze all attention (Q/K/V/O) across all blocks during TTT inner loop, adapt only tok_emb + MLP (fc/proj) layers. Motivated by TTT-E2E (arXiv 2512.23675) finding that freezing attention/embeddings and updating only MLP layers avoids inner-loop instability and improves adaptation quality.

Raise muon_wd (attn group) from 0.095 to 0.105 (~10% UP): attn WD has never been independently probed UP since the attn/MLP split (exp_164); MLP WD moved 0.095→0.125→0.115 (two keeps) while attn stayed at 0.095 — tighter attn weight distributions should improve GPTQ int6 fidelity similarly to how higher MLP WD helped.

ENABLE_LOOPING_AT 0.35 to 0.40 (gentle +14% UP probe on a genuinely untouched UP direction): only prior probe on this axis was exp_536 (DOWN to 0.28, hurt +0.000440); UP direction is freshly unexplored across 870+ trials. Mechanism aligns with SHIFT staged-training principle — give layers 3-5 more single-pass training in the peak-LR window so they build cleaner base representations before the 3x recurrence multiplier engages, shifting bulk of looped-mode training into the warmdown's smoother gradient regime.

Stack chunk-boundary TTT momentum-buffer halving (mul_(0.5) at ci>0, exp_588 technique, Δ=-0.000727 from exp_538 but absent from exp_750 lineage) plus GC row-centering (exp_746, absent from exp_750), removing exp_874's discarded per-epoch cosine LR.

Asymmetric RoPE coverage: looped blocks 3-5 get rope_dims=32 (2× the global default) while non-looped blocks (0-2, 6-10) keep rope_dims=16. Composes with the bifurcated q_gain[H,2] (each block keeps its own per-region gain) and tests whether 3×-replayed iterative-refinement layers benefit from finer per-pass positional discrimination, while non-looped blocks retain NoPE bandwidth for content-key roles.

Add output-side per-feature head_scale [dim=512, init=1.0] applied to x before the tied LM head projection, giving the model independent input/output feature-importance weights under the tied-embedding constraint (distinct from exp_775 which reused the same embed_scale).

Lower muon_wd_mlp 0.115 → 0.108 (−6.1%), continuing exp_336's proven DOWN direction (0.125→0.115 was keep Δ=−0.000109); exp_773 had the same edit but was size_blocked from dirty workdir — clean rebase to exp_750 removes that blocker.

3-level quantile GPTQ damping (correct direction): top-20% H-diag cols get 1.015x, middle 60% keep 1.01x, bottom-20% get 1.005x — iso-mean 1.01 but concentrates ridge on high-importance columns so their error propagates more aggressively to less-important followers.

Replace Latin-square 8-bucket phase cycling with golden-ratio Weyl sequence: phase=int((si+k)*φ%1*max_phase), giving 13 distinct near-optimally-spread phase offsets across 13 shards/rank vs ≥5 duplicate phases under 8-bucket pigeonhole; also saves ~50 packed bytes by removing _phase_order initialization.

Entropy-floor hinge at 4.0 nats stacked on TTT z-loss: 1e-4*F.relu(4-H(p)).mean() where H(p)=lse-sum(p*x), computed cheaply by reusing the lse from z-loss. Threshold=4 nats activates for well-predicted tokens (p1>~60%), adding distribution-shape anti-drift complementary to z-loss's scale control; exp_825's threshold=1 nat was inactive (H>>1 for all typical tokens) producing noise delta of +0.000056.
Re-introduce exp_588's TTT chunk-boundary momentum_buffer halving (mb.mul_(0.5) for ci>0): proven Δ=-0.000727 at exp_538 baseline, lost when lineage forked exp_596 from exp_538 instead of exp_588.

Shorten warmdown_frac from 0.72 to 0.67: expands the stable peak-LR+peak-momentum window from 6% to 11% of the 600s budget (frac 0.22→0.33 vs 0.22→0.28), giving ~155 more steps at full LR for the undertrained model to explore before refinement begins. Linear decay shape preserved unchanged.

ADAM_EPS 1e-8 → 2e-8 (2× UP gentle midpoint between current 1e-8 and exp_802's 1e-7 size_blocked-at-improved-bpb 1.072196): half-step retry of the strongest recent meta UP signal at minimal-step to ease the size cap.

Continuous proportional H damping (exp_818's formula: hd *= 1+0.01*hd/mean(hd), mean=1.01) paired with brotli lgwin=24 (16MB LZ77 window vs default 4MB) to recover the artifact headroom that exp_818 lost when size_blocked.

Raise TTT gradient clip threshold 1.0→2.0: exp_244 showed tighter clip (0.5) hurt BPB by +0.000489, implying the 1.0 clip is actively binding; allowing larger per-step gradient magnitudes should enable more aggressive TTT adaptation.

Switch attn-output gate input from x[..., :12] (pre-attn input) to v.reshape(...,-1)[..., :12] (V projection slice): VGA-style value-state gating per arxiv 2510.09017, byte-neutral (gate_w shape [H=8, W=12] unchanged), single-line forward edit.

Zero WD on attn_out_gate_w: split 88 zero-init per-head sigmoid gate params out of scalar AdamW group into their own sub-group with weight_decay=0.0, letting gradient signal grow them to their true optimum without pull-toward-zero opposition.

Add output-side gauge-fixed out_scale [dim=512, init=1.0] applied INSIDE final_norm (F.rms_norm(x * out_scale,...)), symmetric with the kept input-side embed_scale. Unlike exp_886's failed head_scale (outside norm, absorbable into tok_emb), inside-norm acts directionally and cannot be collapsed into tok_emb.weight learning.

Port exp_186's u² position-weighted CE (pw=0.9+0.2*u², normalized to mean 1.0) onto exp_750 baseline: up-weights high-context late positions by ~14% vs early positions, aligning training gradient with stride-64 sliding-window eval; this technique was kept twice on the exp_164 branch (Δ=−0.000171+−0.000062) but was never applied to the current main lineage (exp_142→exp_196→...→exp_750).

Alternate TTT batch-traversal order across per-chunk epochs (fwd on even, rev on odd): decorrelates SGD gradient directions across the 4 inner epochs, replicating exp_264's proven Δ=-0.000084 keep on a baseline that lacked Nesterov/z-loss — now applying it to exp_750 where it has never been tried.

Damp TTT SGD momentum_buffer by 0.5 ONLY at the first chunk boundary (ci==1) under z-loss regime: surgical port of exp_588 (kept standalone) where uniform damping atop z-loss (exp_607) regressed; chunk-0 builds peak stale velocity at full cos_lr while later boundaries see naturally smaller buildup.</hypothesis> <parameter name="notes">Single 3-line conditional after cos_lr update. +8 packed code bytes. Distinct from exp_588 (all boundaries), exp_607 (all + z-loss, regressed), exp_708 (sign-cond reset), exp_656 (tok_emb-only). Hypothesis: chunk-0 to chunk-1 is the dominant stale-velocity transition; later transitions don't need damping because cos_lr decay reduces velocity buildup naturally and z-loss bounds per-step magnitude. If chunk-0to1 dominates 588's gain, partial recovery (~-0.0003); if late chunks contribute, smaller.

Eval-TTT inner-loop input-token denoising at p=0.01: replace ~1% of input token IDs with uniform-random IDs during TTT inner training forward only; CE target y stays clean. Score-first scoring untouched.

Linearly decay TTT inner-CE z-loss coefficient across 21 epochs: 1e-5 to 1e-5*(1-_ep/h.ttt_epochs). Mechanism-distinct from 9 prior flat-coefficient z-loss variants; front-loads regularization onto fresh-chunk adaptation, gentler as TTT converges.

Per-head row-segment L2 isotropy at tied tok_emb init: split each row D=512 into H=8 head-segments of hd=64, rescale each segment to identical L2 = std*sqrt(64). Removes per-head magnitude noise from finite-N Gaussian sampling while preserving natural row-magnitude variance.
Per-group BETA2 split: lower scalar AdamW beta2 0.95 to 0.93 (h.beta2 to 0.93 literal at L925), keeping optimizer_tok at 0.95. Untouched axis in 700+ trials.

Refit s only on small matrices (rows<1024): exp_747's full-refit ALS scale gave -0.000376 bpb but size_blocked at +891B. Skip the largest scale arrays (tok_emb 8192, mlp_up/gate 2048) which dominated refit's brotli-entropy growth; keep refit on attn proj + mlp.down (rows=512), where weight homogeneity makes refit s less entropy-disruptive.

Zero-init per-KV-head-dim additive V-bias (1D param num_kv_heads*head_dim=256 fp32/block, routes to scalar-AdamW via ndim<2 + TTT-freeze via numel<10000): adds a learnable position-invariant content prior to V after c_v projection, acting as a structural "attention sink" contribution since softmax weights sum to 1. Additive (not scale) — mechanism-distinct from exp_330's failed per-KV-head multiplicative V-gain (size_blocked, 1.075124) and exp_538's multiplicative post-attn per-head gate.

Re-submit exp_595's Muon mm_blend floor 0.25→0.27 (bpb=1.072548 size_blocked by 1,859 B) with ~2,393 B of extra comment trim to clear the 16 MB cap; same weight trajectory as exp_595 should now fit.</hypothesis> <parameter name="notes">exp_595 produced val_bpb=1.072548 (-0.000918 vs best) but the 16.00 MB artifact exceeded the cap by 1,859 bytes. This submit keeps the identical optimizer change (mm_blend = max(lr_scale, 0.27)) and only trims additional verbose comment blocks: module docstring (lines 1-14), attention-backend header + SDPA fallback docstring, muon_momentum_warmup_fraction rationale (lines 153-156), muon_wd_mlp rationale (lines 167-171), TTT section header (lines 176-181), smoke-test gate note (lines 225-228), GPTQ signal-proportional damping rationale (lines 1059-1065), and TTT scalar-freeze rationale (lines 1363-1373). Pack went 26223 → 23830 B (-2393), giving ~534 B safety margin over exp_595's 1859 B overrun. Model trajectory should be bitwise identical since only comments changed; only risk is GPTQ seed variance drifting model_bytes upward.</parameter> </invoke>
Lower per-shard num_sequences cap 2000 to 1500 in _reset_shard for guaranteed full 8-phase coverage

Raise muon_wd (attention matrices only) 0.095 to 0.100 (+5.3% UP): first UP probe on the attn sub-group after the exp_164 type-split. DOWN probes (exp_414 -8% +0.000192, exp_489 -7% size_blocked 1.074149) both failed, leaving UP untested. Small step avoids exp_095's +21% blowup; tightens attn weight norms (GPTQ/brotli-favorable).

Reuse existing embed_scale on the OUTPUT path of the tied softmax: F.linear(x * embed_scale, tok_emb.weight). Zero new params — makes the 512-wide per-feature gain symmetric across the tied tok_emb.weight's two roles (input basis re-weight + output classifier basis re-weight).

Lower muon_momentum_warmup_fraction 0.22 to 0.20 (−9% DOWN): untested gentle-DOWN gap in exp_088's proven faster-ramp direction; prior DOWN probes (0.22 to 0.15/0.16 on older lineages) over-shot, UP probe (exp_515 0.22 to 0.25 discard +0.000114) hurt slightly — optimum may sit at 0.20 under current post-491 stack.

Frequency-stratified per-class CE weight via F.cross_entropy(weight=linspace(0.9,1.1,V)) — upweights rare tokens (high id = rare in SP8192 freq-sorted vocab). Uses PyTorch's built-in weighted reduction='mean' which divides by sum(w[y]) for exact mean-preservation — first clean probe of this axis (exp_518/512 were both size_blocked, exp_272 crashed).

Schedule ema_decay in lockstep with LR warmdown: 0.9965 at peak-LR, rising to 0.998 as scale to 0. Weight-space analog of exp_010/exp_324 (cool Muon momentum during warmdown). exp_393 static 0.998 regressed +0.001989; schedule confines 0.998 to tail only.

Switch GPTQ quantization scale from per-row (W.std(dim=1)) to per-column (W.std(dim=0)): one fp16 scale per INPUT feature instead of per OUTPUT row. Natively aligned with the Hessian-weighted objective (quadratic in the col/input axis) and shrinks tok_emb scale vector from 16 KB → 1 KB (per-row was 8192 fp16, per-col is 512 fp16) — a novel untried axis in the quant tree.
Intra-chunk per-epoch momentum ramp [0.70, 0.83, 0.90, 0.95] on TTT SGD-Nesterov: ep0 low-μ dilutes prior chunk's stale momentum carry-in, later epochs high-μ smooths fresh-chunk grad noise

Loop-equalized TTT LR: looped blocks [3..5] are invoked (num_loops+1)=3× per forward so receive 3× gradient magnitude — scale their TTT SGD base LR by 1/sqrt(num_loops+1)≈0.577 (matching the existing ln_scale_factor variance-balance on those exact blocks) so per-step weight-space drift equalizes across physical layers; untried physical-index LR split (prior exp_133/315/357 matrix-TYPE splits all hurt)
Intra-chunk per-epoch momentum ramp [0.70, 0.83, 0.90, 0.95] on TTT SGD-Nesterov (compacted retry of exp_546 which val_bpb=1.073963 but size_blocked by 394 B over-cap)

Loop-equalized TTT LR (compact): same edit as exp_548 (split TTT SGD into two param groups, scale looped-block LR by 1/sqrt(num_loops+1)) with 255-byte comment/code trim to recover cap headroom; exp_548 packed 81B over (size_blocked) despite val_bpb=1.074048 i.e. −0.000070 vs current best, so the quality signal is real and the only barrier is packing margin
Score-aligned TTT position weights step w_head=0.9 context w_tail=4.1 scored region mean-preserving

Intra-attn Muon WD split: attn.proj (zero-init residual writer) raised 0.095→0.120 while c_q/c_k/c_v stay at 0.095. Mirrors exp_164's kept UP move on mlp-type (0.115 vs 0.095) but scoped within attn; size-safe mirror of exp_483 (which LOWERED mlp.proj WD and size_blocked).

Region-split QK RMSNorm: apply F.rms_norm independently to the RoPE region (first rope_dims=16) and NoPE region (remaining 48 dims) of Q and K, instead of a single joint norm across the whole head_dim. Mirrors exp_475's bifurcated q_gain structure at the normalization layer and removes the implicit coupling where a 16-dim region competes with a 48-dim region for a shared RMS budget.

Half-shard stratification: extend exp_196's largest-remainder per-batch quotas from K=num_files=12 to K=24 virtual shards by splitting each file's sequence range into first/second position-half bins, balancing intra-file position coverage per batch.
Add learnable per-vocab logit_bias to tied-softmax logits before softcap to encode a frequency prior.

Continue GPTQ multiplicative Hessian damping downward trajectory 1.01→1.005 (halve again): exp_280's 1.02 kept (-0.000054), exp_377's 1.01 kept (-0.000084) — each halving improved; at 0.5% damping more true Hessian curvature is preserved for error-propagation while still ridge-regularising high-H columns.

Gentle focal loss (γ=0.25, mean-preserving) replacing plain CE: fw=(1-p_t)^0.25 with detached p_t, normalized to mean 1, so hard tokens contribute more gradient while overall loss/LR scale is unchanged — principled Lin+ 2017 technique, never tried in this tree, orthogonal axis to position-weighting family

Raise tied-embedding Adam weight decay embed_wd 0.085 → 0.095 (+11.8% UP): symmetric probe against exp_041's DOWN attempt (0.085→0.07, size_blocked at bpb=1.078695). UP on embedding WD shrinks row norms → tighter GPTQ int8 codes and better brotli compression on the ~15 MB dominant embedding payload, while also regularizing the tied input/output matrix that serves dual roles.
Lower muon_momentum_warmup_start default 0.92 to 0.90

Shrink exp_493's attn.proj Muon WD bump 0.120→0.110 to fit under 16MB (exp_493 was size_blocked by just 575B while producing val_bpb=1.073997, beating best). Same role-split (q/k/v at 0.095, attn.proj at 0.110, mlp at 0.115) at a smaller delta above baseline, trading a fraction of the WD differentiation for size headroom.

Feature-level F.dropout(p=0.02) on tok_emb output during training only, applied between the embedding lookup and the post-lookup RMSNorm (gauge-fix site): regularizes the INPUT use of the tied-embedding matrix (sparse lookup, more overfit-prone than the dense tied readout) without perturbing the output-tied gradient path. p=0.02 is 2.5x gentler than exp_040's failed MLP-hidden p=0.05 and at a structurally upstream site. Zero params, zero bytes.

Multi-Token Prediction auxiliary CE (α=0.05): add a small-weight CE term on target[t+1] (two-steps-ahead token) alongside the standard next-token CE, forcing the rep at t to encode near-future signal. Training-only, same tied head, zero new params.
Lower adam_wd 0.005→0.002 (2.5× further DOWN) on scalar-params AdamW group — direct continuation of exp_177's proven DOWN direction (0.02→0.005, Δ=−0.000038).

Per-chunk Nesterov momentum-buffer reset in eval_val_ttt: zero state['momentum_buffer'] for all TTT params at each chunk boundary so inherited stale direction from early high-LR chunks on different-distribution data doesn't leak into late low-LR chunks; keeps proven within-chunk μ=0.9 Nesterov smoothing (exp_151) while discarding inter-chunk carry-over (orthogonal lever from exp_472's gradual μ cooling 0.9→0.5, which discarded).
Raise peak Muon momentum 0.99 to 0.992: untried PEAK momentum axis

Floor TTT cosine LR at 0.1×ttt_lr instead of decaying to 0 (mirrors exp_324's Muon-momentum warmdown-floor win on opt side): keeps late chunks doing small-LR adaptation so the last ~20% of chunks still contribute gradient before scoring their successors.

Intra-MLP Muon WD split: mlp.fc stays at muon_wd_mlp=0.115 (feature-creating, silu²-squashed), mlp.proj drops to muon_wd_mlp_proj=0.095 to match attn's WD — structural parity between the two zero-init residual-writers (attn.proj + mlp.proj) while preserving firm WD on the feature-creating path.

Replace within-shard random permutation with a golden-ratio LCG order (stride≈0.618·N, coprime with N, per-epoch random offset): deterministic low-discrepancy sequence ordering, untried permutation-STRUCTURE axis orthogonal to exp_474's block-B=8 (locality) and exp_047's half-stride (overlap).

Apply logit temperature T=0.97 (3% sharpening, divide logits by 0.97) inside eval_val_ttt scoring pass only — symmetric opposite of exp_044 (T=1.05 softening → Δ=+0.002607) on the hypothesis that GPTQ-int6 flattens output distributions and optimal T<1.

Alternate Weyl multiplier across shard-epochs (golden φ⁻¹≈0.618 on even epochs, silver √2−1≈0.414 on odd) in _reset_shard to decorrelate consecutive-epoch within-shard visit orders: both irrationals give valid low-discrepancy sequences, and silver ≠ 1−golden so the two orders are structurally different (not just reverses).

Swap F.rms_norm → F.layer_norm at the post-tok_emb / embed_scale gauge-fix site: adds mean-centering alongside variance-normalization, stripping any DC/unigram direction from tok_emb rows before the residual is frozen as x0 and replicated through every decoder via skip_weights.

Recurrence-aware x0-INJECTION correction (orthogonal to exp_245): for looped blocks [3..5], scale the mix[1]*x0 embedding-injection term in Block.forward by 1/(num_loops+1)=1/3 so the per-forward TOTAL x0 contribution of a looped block equals that of a single-call block; exp_245 damps attn/MLP OUTPUTS via ln_scale_factor but leaves the residual-stream x0 injection at 3× magnitude.
Cosine-cool TTT SGD momentum 0.9 to 0.5 in lockstep with cosine LR schedule over chunks.

Tighten muon_momentum_warmup_fraction 0.22→0.15: shortens the 0.92→0.99 ramp so peak momentum lands earlier and the peak-momentum × peak-LR coupling window grows from ~6% to ~13% of budget — never-probed DOWN direction on this axis (exp_029 only tested UP to 0.45, harness_abort).

Lower muon_wd_qk 0.115 → 0.095 (match attn c_v/proj group): Q/K outputs are RMS-normed before SDPA so row-norms are absorbed, making MLP-matched WD over-regularize direction diversity. Symmetric DOWN test mirroring exp_336's MLP WD win.

Exclude the looped blocks [loop_start..loop_end] from the TTT optimizer — they are called (num_loops+1)=3 times per forward, so their TTT backward gradient accumulates 3× signal (same recurrence amplification exp_141 and exp_245 compensated); freezing them during TTT focuses adaptation on unique-role layers (blocks 0-2, 6-10) + embedding/norms/scalars and avoids 3×-amplified TTT noise on the shared-weight recurrent core.

Re-enable mild label_smoothing=0.002 on the current exp_324 baseline's plain CE — a midpoint between the known-good old-default 0.003 and the known-worse 0.001 (from exp_042 regression), untested on the current stack (QK-gain 5.25 + embed_scale-inside-RMSNorm + Muon-mom-floor + TTT). Also motivates TTT stability: softer train distribution is a better-conditioned init for per-chunk inner-loop SGD.

Position-weighted Hessian accumulation in GPTQ calibration: scale each token's activation by sqrt(linspace(0.9,1.1,T)) before x.T@x so the Hessian emphasises late-context positions that dominate the stride-64 sliding-window eval — mean-preserving (trace unchanged), size-neutral (calibration only), mirrors exp_176's proven 0.9→1.1 training-CE weighting.

Add output-side per-feature bias output_bias_feat (shape=[embedding_dim]=512, init=0) applied to x via F.linear(x + output_bias_feat, tok_emb.weight) before the tied softmax — structural DUAL of input-side embed_scale and mathematically a rank-1 per-vocab logit bias (W @ b) that captures frequency-prior capacity at 512 params instead of the full 8192-dim vocab_bias that size-blocked exp_023/146/166/219/249/277/317.

Apply T=0.97 logit sharpening at eval_val_ttt scoring site (divide logits by 0.97 before CE); exp_044 showed T=1.05 (smoothing) hurt +0.0026 so optimum lies at T<=1.0, and GPTQ weight noise widens output entropy — mild sharpening should recover calibration. TTT training loss untouched.
Skip-path RMSNorm: F.rms_norm the popped encoder skip to unit RMS BEFORE skip_weights multiplication, decoupling skip_weights (pure per-feature gain) from skip_gates (pure mix fraction).

Exempt per-channel/per-head GAIN params (q_gain=5.25-init, attn_scale/mlp_scale/embed_scale/skip_weights=1.0-init) from adam_wd by splitting the scalar AdamW group into a zero-WD "gain" sub-group and a 0.005-WD "gate" sub-group (resid_mix, skip_gates).

Raise Muon peak momentum 0.99→0.993: widens effective gradient-averaging window from ~100 to ~143 steps. Parallel axis to exp_088 (ramp speed, -0.001115 keep) and exp_324 (warmdown shape, -0.000202 keep) — the peak value itself is the only momentum knob never tuned.
Latin-square phase count 8→5 (prime, coprime with seq_len=2^11): 3-reset saturation 37.5%→60%, phase spacing 256→410 tokens (adjacent-phase content overlap 88%→80%).

Raise matrix_lr 0.022 → 0.023 (+4.5%): half-step in the less-bad direction on a now-untested axis — exp_214 (0.024, +0.000180) hurt less than exp_224 (0.020, +0.000550), and several kept downstream changes (exp_245/264/280/298) have shifted the regime since the last matrix_lr probe.

Double Latin-square phase bucket count 8→16 in ShuffledSequenceLoader: finer within-shard phase granularity (128-token spacing vs 256-token) on the initial-phase draw, which is the dominant curr lever since shards don't exhaust in a 600s run. exp_207 tested the opposite direction (8→4) and lost +0.00085; the increase direction is orthogonal and untested.

Reset Nesterov SGD momentum buffer at each chunk boundary in eval_val_ttt: zero state['momentum_buffer'] for all ttt_params right after updating cos_lr, before inner epoch loop. Isolates each chunk's adaptation from prior chunks' gradient history (val data is not i.i.d. across chunks, and cosine-decaying LR means stale momentum from chunk 0's high-LR gradients disproportionately biases chunk N's small-LR updates).

Re-introduce exp_186's proven u^2 convex position-weighted CE (pw = (0.9 + 0.2*u^2) / mean, mean-preserving) onto exp_298 baseline — position weighting was stripped from current best, and u^2 spreads the +0.2 tilt smoothly across all T vs sigmoid/hard-mask variants that concentrate it on ~64–300 tail positions and keep hitting size_blocked by only 0.9–2.8 KB.

Soft eval-aligned TTT loss: mean-preserving 1.5x upweight on the last stride=64 positions of each TTT training seq, keeping unit weight on the first 1984. Interpolates between current unweighted TTT (alpha=0) and exp_322's hard-mask failure (alpha=infinity, +0.0117 regression).

Per-KV-head learnable V-gain (shape=[num_kv_heads]=4 per layer, init=1.0) applied right after c_v projection to complete the Q-K-V gain trio: QK-Gain already sharpens attention scores; V-gain decouples attention direction from output magnitude per head, letting heads specialize by how much they read rather than just where.

Swap tied tok_emb init from nn.init.normal_ to nn.init.trunc_normal_ at ±2σ (PaLM/T5/BLOOM/Llama standard): clips the ~5% of rare-token rows beyond ±2σ=±0.01 that, under tied embeddings, double as outlier softmax classification directions and can create spurious early-grad spikes.</hypothesis> <parameter name="notes">Single-line edit in GPT._init_weights: nn.init.normal_(mean=0, std=0.005) -> nn.init.trunc_normal_(mean=0, std=0.005, a=-0.01, b=0.01). Zero params added, zero bytes delta; model pack approximately exp_298. Mechanistic rationale: with tied embeddings the tok_emb rows ARE the output softmax classifier rows, so the Gaussian tail mass (~5% of 8192 rows beyond ±2σ) creates rare-token classifiers whose initial direction is dominated by the random outlier, forcing the first hundred grad steps to correct artifacts before real signal can be absorbed. Truncating the tails at ±2σ removes the outliers while keeping the bulk of the distribution and its natural per-row variance intact — distinct from exp_019's equi-row-norm sphere init (discarded, +0.001603) which forced EVERY row to identical L2 norm and destroyed natural row-magnitude variation; this keeps in-bulk variance and only removes outliers. First-try attempt at this direction: exp_032 proposed trunc_normal at ±2σ but was harness_abort'd (never reached GPU), so the hypothesis has not been disproven. Zero risk of size_blocked (no param delta) or crash (stdlib nn.init). Standard practice in PaLM/T5/BLOOM/Llama adoption of trunc_normal is specifically to prevent pre-training outlier classifiers under tied embeddings.</notes> </invoke>
Shrink GPTQ block_size 64 to 16 in gptq_quantize_weight: 4x more Hessian-compensated error-propagation events per matrix than the exp_298 default (which still has block_size=64).

Time-anneal Muon weight decay: WD=0 for first 15% of budget (exploration), linearly ramp 0→1 from 15% to warmdown-start (42%), then full per-group base WD throughout the warmdown tail — concentrates regularization pressure on the settled-feature + EMA-warmdown regime where overfit risk peaks.

Within-shard positional augmentation: replace stride-seq_len grid of start_inds (phase + k*2048) with uniform random integer starts over [phase, num_tokens - seq_len - 1], keeping Latin-square phase and sequence count — scatters doc-boundary splice points at ~50K-token resolution instead of on a 2048-grid, with negligible duplicate risk (~0.04 birthday bound per shard-reset in a 600s run).

Eval-aligned last-stride CE weighting: 1.15× boost on the rightmost 64 positions (= eval sliding stride), mean-preserving — concentrates training gradient on the exact positions that contribute NEW logits to val_bpb after window 0.

Per-category GPTQ Hessian damping: MLP weights get H_ii *= 1.025 (silu^2 activations, heavy-tailed bimodal diag(H)), attn/embed stay at 1.015 (post-RMSNorm ~Gaussian inputs).
