Motivation: every quant-damping trial tuned amplitude globally; no one has tried per-layer routing. MLP-proj inputs are silu^2(fc(x)) — non-negative, heavy tail, large diag(H) dynamic range on sink columns where 1/Hinv[j,j] amplifies — benefits from more damping. Attn QKV sees post-RMSNorm ~Gaussian — lighter damping preserves curvature fidelity. Embed (tied head) sees final_norm output → also Gaussian-ish → attn-style. Zero byte overhead.</notes> </invoke>

Additive Gaussian feature noise (σ=0.02) on the re-injected x0 anchor path only (training-only), not on the primary residual stream x: noise propagates to every block via resid_mix[1]*x0 but is self-gated by the learned mix[1] (init=0), sidestepping the branch-kill/zero-gate failure mode of exp_040 (MLP hidden dropout) and exp_293 (MLP DropPath).

Muon-only sqrt warmdown: apply muon_lr_scale = lr_scale**0.5 to the Muon param group while Adam (tok/head/scalar) groups retain linear warmdown. Rationale: Muon's NS-5 orthogonalization emits unit-SV updates, so group LR ≈ step-norm in weight space; the convex sqrt shape keeps Muon hotter through the tail (at lr_scale=0.1 Muon sees 0.316× vs linear's 0.1×) while Adam groups, whose effective step is raw-grad-dependent, keep the proven linear shape. Momentum cool-during-warmdown is unchanged (still driven by the global linear lr_scale). Zero bytes, zero params, single-site edit in step_fn. Not previously tried: shape changes have only been tested as global (exp_002 cosine keep, exp_043 cosine discard); asymmetric-per-group warmdown is unexplored.

Lower adam_eps 1e-8 → 1e-9 (single literal, all three AdamW groups share this): 10× step vs exp_048/exp_105 which both went 1e-10 and failed on size/eval-budget, so the training signal on this axis is genuinely unobserved. Directionally aligned with the 4-of-4 β2-raise discards (exp_068/091/126/159), which say the current stack wants a SHARPER preconditioner, not smoother — smaller eps sharpens the denominator for low-second-moment rows (rare-tail tok_emb, scalar ln_scale/qk_gain/skip_gates).

Lookahead slow-weights pullback at each TTT chunk boundary: initialize slow_params to pre-TTT snapshot, then after every chunk's SGD steps do slow ← slow + 0.5·(live − slow) followed by live ← slow. Structurally distinct from per-step L2 anchor to pre-TTT (exp_276 discard, −0.0003) and hard momentum reset (exp_253/255/263 discard) because (a) it operates on parameters not gradient/momentum, (b) the anchor point drifts cumulatively instead of being fixed to pre-TTT, and (c) the pullback cadence is per-chunk not per-step — so early-chunk adaptation is preserved in slow_params while per-chunk overshoot is halved.

Output-side companion to exp_245: scale attn_scale/mlp_scale INIT of looped blocks (layers 3-5) by 1/sqrt(num_loops+1)=1/sqrt(3) so each of the 3 per-pass residual contributions starts at ~58% of a non-looped block — variance-balances the effective-depth-17 residual stream from the OUTPUT side on top of exp_245's INPUT-side ln_scale_factor damping.

Defer chunk 0 scoring to the END of the TTT loop so chunk 0 scores with a fully-adapted model instead of zero-TTT benefit — reorder chunk_order to [1,2,...,N-1,0] and re-anchor cos_lr on loop index, preserving score-first invariant (chunk 0 tokens never trained on).

Multi-token-prediction auxiliary CE (DeepSeek-V3 style, α=0.1) reusing the tied output head: at each position t, the same logits are scored against target[t+1] as a 0.1-weighted aux term on top of the main next-token CE. Zero bytes / zero params / train-only (reuses tied tok_emb head, no new projection). Structurally orthogonal to the saturated position-weighted, rare-token, focal, label-smoothing, and entropy-bonus families.

Decouple Muon momentum warmdown-end from warmup-start: add muon_momentum_warmdown_end=0.85 (vs the previous implicit 0.92) so during the 72% warmdown phase, momentum cools 0.99→0.85 instead of 0.99→0.92; extends exp_010's cool-during-warmdown trajectory by another 7 pts, making end-of-run low-LR updates behave more like fine-tune SGD with less stale-direction carryover.

Target-decay on scalar AdamW group: replace decoupled WD's pull-toward-0 with pull-toward-init for q_gain (5.25), attn_scale/mlp_scale/resid_mix[0]/skip_weights/embed_scale (1.0), and zero-init resid_mix[1]/skip_gates (0.0) — preserves exp_177's proven stabilizing drag (adam_wd=0.005) while redirecting it to each param's meaningful attractor.

Widen Muon momentum ramp on its untested side: muon_momentum_warmup_start 0.92→0.90 (symmetric opposite of exp_015's failed 0.92→0.94 narrowing, re-probed under the current post-exp_088 fast-ramp regime).
Shrink TTT training micro-batch from 32 to 2 in eval_val_ttt, doubling SGD steps per chunk (4 to 8) and making exp_264 alternating fwd/rev actually permute.

Sandwich normalisation: RMSNorm the attn and MLP branch OUTPUTS (before attn_scale/mlp_scale) on top of the existing pre-norm inputs — direction-preserving output-magnitude discipline for the effective-depth-17 recurrent residual stream.

Add learnable per-feature embed_bias (shape=[embedding_dim], init=0.0) as the additive companion to exp_267's embed_scale — completes the post-tok_emb pipeline into full affine-LayerNorm form (rms_norm × scale + bias), letting the residual-skip anchor x0 carry a learned absolute-feature offset that survives the first block's pre-norm.

Deranged per-shard phase assignment: replace exp_142's 8-phase latin-square (with K=13 shards per rank, has ~99% prob of same-phase collisions) with a K-bucket derangement - each shard gets a UNIQUE phase index in [0..K-1], spacing shard-starting-offsets at 2048/K=158-token granularity and eliminating within-rank phase duplication.

Disable XSA on looped blocks [3,4,5] only (pre-loop 0-2 and post-loop 6-10 keep XSA): XSA's V-direction subtraction compounds across the 3 loop passes of each physical block, over-damping attention; loop_seg benefits from standard attention while non-loop layers retain XSA sharpening.

Per-shard independent reset on exhaustion in ShuffledSequenceLoader.next_batch: when shard si runs dry mid-batch, reset only si (not all shards), preserving remaining-but-unused sequences in the other shards and letting each shard's _phase_epoch advance at its own rate proportional to shard size.
Per-head multiplicative noise on q_gain (±2% uniform) during training only — mean-preserving DropHead-style reg on attention sharpness, zero-param, zero-byte.

Position-decaying label smoothing eps(u) = 0.01*(1-u) across T=2048: smooths early-position targets (inherently high-entropy, no context) while keeping late positions (where sliding-window stride-64 eval scores) sharp. Mean eps ~0.005; zero train/val mismatch at t=T-1.

Per-sequence fine-grained positional jitter in [0,32) added to each training start_ind inside ShuffledSequenceLoader._reset_shard: multiplies unique starting positions per shard pass by ~32x on top of the existing 8-phase Latin-square (256-token coarse spacing), without altering sample counts, model params, or artifact size.
Split Muon attention WD by projection role: raise WD on V + output projections (c_v + attn.proj) to 0.125, keep Q+K at 0.095. Attention-internal analogue of exp_164.
Double GPTQ calibration batches 64→128: halves Hessian estimator variance so H^{-1} error-propagation in the GPTQ inner loop tracks true second-order curvature more precisely.

Decouple Muon-momentum warmdown floor from warmup_start and raise it 0.92 to 0.95 (softer lockstep cool during warmdown); exp_109 lowered coupled floor to 0.85 (discard) and exp_200 tried kinked hold-then-drop cool (discard) — shallower smooth-cool direction is untested.
Lower tied_embed_lr 0.03 to 0.026 on tied tok_emb AdamW group.

Reset SGD momentum_buffer at each TTT chunk boundary so stale velocity from chunk (i-1) — scaled by prior cos_lr — doesn't contaminate chunk i's first steps under a different LR; each chunk's ~16 SGD steps start from clean velocity.

Reset TTT SGD momentum buffer at the start of each chunk's training phase: stale momentum from earlier high-LR chunks pushes updates in directions misaligned with the cosine LR decay in late low-LR chunks, effectively overriding the schedule.

Reset SGD momentum buffer at each TTT chunk boundary (optimizer.state = defaultdict(dict)) so each chunk adapts locally rather than inheriting stale momentum from prior chunks' (different-distribution) gradients.

Raise matrix_lr (Muon LR on ~29M matrix params) 0.022 → 0.024 (+9%): completely untouched core LR knob across ~200 trials; Muon's Newton-Schulz normalization bounds spectral-norm updates, so LR bumps translate to proportionally larger but still-stable steps during the narrow ~6% peak-LR+peak-momentum window.
Disable XSA on looped layers [loop_start..loop_end] only: repeated V-direction subtraction across num_loops+1=3 passes over-whitens iterative refinement; non-looped layers keep XSA.

Anti-focal CE reweighting (γ=0.5): multiply existing u² PW by w = exp(-ce.detach()/2) (= p_correct^0.5), mean-preserving, stacked on the current u²-PW-CE. Tests the symmetric opposite of the failed focal-γ=0.5 (exp_054, +0.002535) and focal-γ=1 (exp_144, +0.009038) — downweight near-floor hard tokens so gradient concentrates on reducible-loss positions.

Mask first 64 positions of target_ids with ignore_index=-100 in training CE (forward), aligning training regime with sliding-window eval which dominantly scores positions with ≥1984 tokens of prior context (eval stride=64).

Constant-effective-WD on the tied tok_emb during warmdown: boost optimizer_tok weight_decay by 1/max(lr_scale, 0.5) (cap 2x) in step_fn so the per-step decay factor (1 - lr*wd) on tok_emb stays roughly constant through warmdown — tightens the tied embedding (which doubles as output head) exactly when LR is cooling.</hypothesis> <parameter name="notes">Single-site edit in train_model.step_fn right after the LR-scaling loop. Zero new params, no arch change. In baseline, effective embed-WD per step (lr*wd) decays proportionally to lr_scale during warmdown. This edit keeps it roughly constant for the first half of warmdown (lr_scale in [1.0, 0.5]) then clamps (wd_boost=2x) for the second half. Target: compress tied-embedding weight norms more aggressively in the warmdown tail for better generalization AND better brotli compressibility on the output-head int6 codes. Novel reg direction — prior reg keeps (exp_141 depth-split Muon-WD, exp_164 type-split Muon-WD) both targeted Muon matrix groups; this is the first scheduled AdamW-WD intervention, orthogonal to them. Skeptical case: if equilibrium embed norm shrinks too much, softmax logit magnitudes drop and TTT can't recover them — hence the conservative 2x cap (not 4x).

Add learnable per-vocab logit bias (8192 fp32 scalars, zero-init, passthrough fp16): decouples output frequency prior from tied tok_emb rows that otherwise must serve as both compositional-meaning vectors AND likelihood-prior vectors. Added before tanh softcap; trained via scalar AdamW group (scalar_lr=0.02, adam_wd=0.02).

Shorten warmdown_frac 0.72→0.65: reclaim 7% of budget (42s) as peak-LR learning — extends the peak-momentum×peak-LR overlap window from 36s to 78s (exp_088's proven-valuable regime), while 390s warmdown tail still gives ~15 EMA half-lives for convergence.

Label-smoothing ε=0.02 applied ONLY to TTT-training CE (scoring + main training unchanged): regularizes the per-chunk 4-epoch SGD updates against overconfident chunk-specific n-gram memorization that would bias subsequent-chunk scoring.

Restrict XSA to the decoder half only: xsa_last_n 11→6 (apply Exclusive-Self-Attention exactly to layers 5..10 = num_decoder_layers). XSA subtracts each head's V-direction from its output (an anti-self regularizer); on encoder layers this may damage feature construction while on decoder layers it productively forces attention onto other positions. exp_028 tried xsa_last_n=5 but was harness_aborted (never tested); 6 is the clean encoder/decoder cut aligned with num_encoder_layers=11//2=5.

Soft row-L2 cap at tied tok_emb init: after nn.init.normal_, shrink only the outlier ~5% of rows whose L2 norm exceeds 1.15×expected_norm (=1.15·std·sqrt(d)) down to exactly that cap; the other ~95% of rows keep their natural Gaussian row-norm variation — preserves the log-unigram prior exp_019's strict sphere init destroyed, while killing the long tail that drives per-row int8 quant scales and early-training gradient noise.
Tighten TTT per-step grad clip 1.0→0.5: pairs with exp_151's Nesterov (kept) by capping outlier lookahead-amplified updates on anomalous val chunks while keeping clip 1.67x training's 0.3.

Halve eval_stride 64→32: doubles scoring-window density so every scored token gets avg 2031.5 context tokens (up from 2015.5, +0.79%), applied uniformly to both quantized_sliding_window and quantized_ttt scoring passes.

Raise AdamW beta2 0.95→0.98 for the tok_emb optimizer only (largest Adam-managed tensor, 4.2M params, Zipfian heavy-tailed grads under tied embeddings) — more stable second-moment denominator on rare-token directions; scalar/head groups keep 0.95.

Per-module GPTQ clip_sigmas: tighten attn matrices (c_q/c_k/c_v/attn.proj) to 12.0 while keeping MLP/other at 12.85. Captures the bpb win of exp_165 (size_blocked at global 12.0) while limiting the entropy hit to ~27% of matrix params so size stays under 16MB.

Per-sequence ±64-token start_ind jitter in ShuffledSequenceLoader._reset_shard decorrelates training windows from the strict phase+k*seq_len grid that persists in 600s runs (where _reset_shard only fires once per shard), matching eval's stride-64 sliding-window resolution without incurring exp_047's overlap penalty.
Depth-targeted MLP Muon WD: loop-segment MLPs (layers 3-5, used 3x/forward) at muon_wd_mlp_recurrent=0.155, non-loop MLPs at 0.125

Pair brotli lgwin=22→24 (max 16 MB compression window vs default 4 MB) with modest matrix_clip_sigmas 12.85→12.0 tightening — the larger window captures cross-matrix redundancy in the ~27 MB quant dict to fund finer GPTQ int6 resolution without exceeding the 16 MB cap.

Exclude Muon matrix params from global grad_clip_norm — Newton-Schulz already bounds per-layer Muon update spectrum, so global L2 clipping couples Muon grads to sparse tok_emb/lm_head outliers and damps them unnecessarily; clip only Adam-managed (tok_emb + lm_head + scalar/control) params at the same 0.3 threshold.

Tighten matrix_clip_sigmas 12.85→10.5 — direct SDClip scale tightening (18% smaller quant scale → finer int6 resolution for bulk Gaussian weights) without the aggressive per-row MinMSE that size-blocked exp_145/exp_004/exp_008.

Add uniform intra-bucket jitter to exp_142's Latin-square phase cycling: each reset samples jitter∈[0,bucket_width) on top of the bucket offset so repeat visits to the same bucket k land on a DIFFERENT actual phase. Preserves Latin-square coverage guarantee at bucket granularity while extending phase coverage from 8 discrete points to continuous over [0,max_phase).

Continuous input-embedding Gaussian noise σ=0.015 added to post-RMSNorm x in forward_logits, train-only via self.training gate — a smoother alternative to exp_040's failed MLP hidden dropout and exp_012's size-blocked discrete token-replacement.

Gentle position-weighted CE (linspace 0.9→1.1 across T=2048, mean-preserving): aligns training gradient with eval's stride-64 scored regime [1984,2047] by a 20% amplitude tilt toward late positions, structurally distinct from prior per-token (exp_144 focal), target-side (exp_042 smoothing), and symmetric (exp_009 z-loss) loss trials.

Add a learnable per-vocab output bias (8192 fp32 scalars) applied AFTER logit softcap: logits = softcap*tanh(F.linear(x, tok_emb.weight)/softcap) + out_bias, zero-init, in scalar_params optimizer group. Decouples the log-unigram/marginal-frequency prior from tied tok_emb rows so embedding geometry specializes on token direction rather than absorbing both direction + frequency scale.

Eval-stride-aligned sigmoid position weight: mean-preserving S-curve centered at t=T-64 with slope 1/16 and amplitude 0.3, concentrating the gradient tilt at the exact eval-stride boundary (bulk ≈0.99, tail peaks ≈1.29) instead of exp_156's uniform linspace(0.9,1.1) spread.

Pure sliding-window semantics: first window (ws=0) now scores only positions [context_size:wlen] like all subsequent windows, eliminating low-context warmup tokens (positions 0..1983) from val_bpb so every scored token has uniform >=1984-token context (HF-style perplexity convention).

Tighten grad_clip_norm 0.3→0.2 (−33%): the 3-layer recurrent segment 3×-amplifies gradient norms in loop layers so the current 0.3 clip attenuates them proportionally less than non-recurrent layers — tighter clipping complements exp_141's recurrent-WD hike on the gradient side.
Fatter heads: num_heads 8→4, num_kv_heads 4→2 (head_dim 64→128, param-neutral), rope_dims 32→64 to preserve exp_005's 50% partial-RoPE coverage under the new head size.

Reset SGD momentum buffers at each TTT chunk boundary (clear optimizer.state['momentum_buffer'] after setting cos_lr): each chunk is a distinct text region, and with only ~8-16 SGD steps/chunk at momentum=0.9 the tail velocity from the previous chunk biases the early updates of the new chunk.

Ramp Muon weight_decay 1.0x->1.3x linearly with warmdown (lr_scale 1->0): compensates for lr*wd effective-decay collapse in the tail and drives Muon matrices toward smaller magnitudes for better int6 GPTQ fit — mirrors exp_141's 1.3x muon_wd hike but applied globally as a time-fractional schedule.</hypothesis> <parameter name="notes">Single-site insertion (~7 lines) in step_fn between Muon momentum cool and the lr_scale loop. No new env var. Sets muon_wd_cur = h.muon_wd * (1.0 + (1.0-lr_scale)*0.3) every step and writes it into optimizer_muon.param_groups. Rationale: Muon's per-step decay p*=(1-lr*wd) naturally drops as lr->0 during warmdown, so the regularization strength collapses just when int6 GPTQ quantization error (which scales with weight magnitude) matters most for val_bpb. exp_141 (reg, kept at delta=-0.000274) already proved 1.3x muon_wd helps on recurrent blocks; extending the proven magnitude as a time-fractional schedule across ALL Muon matrices. Distinct from exp_163 (beta1 cool, discarded delta=+0.000146) since wd acts on weight magnitudes, not gradient moments, and targets quantization error directly. Active only during warmdown (lr_scale<1.0); peak phase unchanged.

Round-robin stratified shard sampling in next_batch (with per-batch random rotation offset) replaces weighted-random independent per-slot draws, eliminating per-batch shard-composition multinomial variance (~50% CoV with 12 shards × 48 slots).

Temperature sharpening T=0.97 on scoring logits in eval_val_ttt (divide logits by 0.97 before CE) — opposite direction of exp_044 (T=1.05 worsened +0.00261), motivated by GPTQ int6 weight noise flattening the output distribution.

Zipf-weighted row-magnitude init for tied tok_emb: after normal_ init, multiply row i by 1/sqrt(1 + i/1024) so rare high-ID tokens start with smaller norms. Tied embeddings feed F.linear for output logits (row-norm-sensitive), so rare-token logits start small → low-confidence uniform prior for rares at t=0, matching BPB's objective.
Bump Adam beta2 0.95→0.98: tied embedding (4.2M params) sees sparse per-row gradients; longer 2nd-moment half-life (~35 vs ~14 steps) stabilizes Adam's preconditioner for long-tail vocab rows.

Restore orphaned exp_005 win: rope_dims 16→32 (50% partial RoPE). exp_005 proved -0.000788 on the older stack but the change was lost at exp_014 when a sibling specialist rebased from a pre-exp_005 ancestor; every subsequent keep (including current best exp_142) inherits rope_dims=16.

Init resid_mix[1] (x0/embedding injection scale) from 0.0→0.05 per-layer: give every block a weak embedding-bypass channel from step 0 so gradient has informative signal immediately; with 3-layer recurrence (layers 3-5 called 3x) every loop pass sees x0 re-injected consistently from the start.

Focal-style (γ=1) per-token CE reweighting, mean-normalized: w=(1-p_correct) under no_grad, then normalized so focal_w.mean()=1 to preserve overall gradient magnitude (LR schedule stays calibrated). Redirects gradient budget from saturated easy tokens (common function words, trailing punctuation) toward content tokens where ~36M-param capacity is information-limited.

Replace GPTQ per-row σ-based SDClip scale (clip_sigmas*row_std/clip_range) with max-abs scale (row.abs().amax()/clip_range): uses full int6/int8 range for every row with zero clipping, ~3× finer quantization bins on non-outlier rows where current clip_sigmas=12.85σ wastes ~70% of int6 range.

Add a learnable 8192-dim output logit bias applied AFTER the tanh softcap: frees tied tok_emb rows from encoding unigram frequency via their norms (unconstrained log-prior shifts live in the bias), moving that capacity to directional/semantic content while the softcap still bounds the inner-product term.

Cosine-cool TTT SGD momentum 0.9→0 across chunks in lockstep with the existing cos_lr schedule, so late low-LR chunks effectively do vanilla SGD and the momentum buffer can't carry stale early-chunk grads into the precision-adaptation tail.

Shrink warmdown_frac 0.72→0.60 to expand the peak-LR+peak-momentum window 6%→18% of training — frac=[0.22,0.40] at peak-LR+peak-momentum instead of only [0.22,0.28], tripling the window exp_088 opened by shrinking Muon momentum warmup 0.33→0.22.

Decouple muon_momentum_warmdown_end (0.85) from muon_momentum_warmup_start (0.92): exp_010 cools Muon momentum 0.99→warmup_start during warmdown, locking the warmdown floor to 0.92. Lowering the floor to 0.85 makes deep-warmdown steps less momentum-dragged (fresher per-step gradient), letting the EMA averager (decay 0.9965, half-life ~200 steps) do more of the smoothing across the 72% warmdown tail — a Polyak-Ruppert shift of averaging from intra-step momentum to cross-step EMA.

Lower muon_wd 0.095→0.065 (~32% reduction, nanoGPT-speedrun-aligned): untouched knob on ~28M Muon-managed matrix params. Under exp_088's earlier-peak momentum ramp (0.99 reached before frac=0.28), the smoothed gradient buffer makes decoupled weight-decay more-effective per step, so the baseline 0.095 likely overshoots — less WD should recover generalization headroom in the warmdown tail without destabilizing peak-LR training.

Poly-1 loss with eps=-1.0 (L = CE + eps*(1-p_t)) — gradient on target logit scales by (1-p_t), acting as focal-γ=1 hard-example mining; directs training signal toward structurally hard tokens where an underfit 36M model has slack.

Stratified (low-discrepancy) within-shard start indices: partition [0, max_start] into num_sequences equal bins, draw one uniform start per bin, then shuffle — gives minimum-spacing ≈ seq_len with no bin-collisions, sitting between packed (zero in-bin variance) and IID (Poisson clumping).

Reset SGD momentum buffer at each TTT chunk boundary — stale momentum direction from prior chunk's gradients is distributionally misaligned with new chunk's text, and with ~4 SGD steps/chunk at momentum=0.9 the prior chunk's first-step gradient still carries 66% weight into the new chunk's first step.

Extend partial-RoPE coverage rope_dims 32→48 (75% of head_dim=64, leaving 16 dims NoPE): continues exp_005's kept trajectory (16→32, Δ=-0.000788) at zero parameter cost, giving attention more positional signal while retaining some NoPE dims for position-invariant semantic matching.

Introduce a learnable scalar logit_gain (init=1.0) multiplied into logits pre-softcap so the scalar-optimizer group can grow output magnitude without pulling on embed_wd=0.085 which compresses tied tok_emb rows for input regularization.

Sharpen scoring logits with T=0.95 (divide by 0.95) in eval_val_ttt score-first pass only; counters GPTQ int6 noise that flattens logit distribution. exp_044 showed T=1.05 smoothing HURT by +0.0026 — the opposite direction is untested.

Targeted Muon weight-decay hike on c_q/c_k projections only (muon_wd_qk=0.15 vs global muon_wd=0.095); attention sharpness is already handled by learnable q_gain=5.25, so containing Q,K magnitudes specifically regularizes attention without shrinking V/O or MLP capacity — orthogonal to the global muon_wd=0.115 hike in exp_095 that hurt everything uniformly.

Reset TTT SGD momentum buffers at chunk boundaries (not across): each val chunk is a non-stationary distribution, so velocity from chunk i opposes chunk i+1's gradient; momentum still accumulates across the 4 inner epochs on the same chunk.

Add Sub-LN (post-attention and post-MLP RMSNorm, no-affine) inside Block.forward: decouples direction from magnitude so attn_scale/mlp_scale purely control amplitude while F.rms_norm bounds residual-stream growth, especially valuable under 3-layer recurrence where blocks 3-5 are reused 3x.

Raise Adam beta2 0.95→0.98 (canonical LLM default): 2nd-moment EMA half-life grows ~14→~34 steps, smoothing the preconditioner denominator for tied tok_emb (4.2M params) and scalar groups without touching Muon; unexplored static tweak (only cosine-scheduled beta2 was tried in exp_021→crash; sibling exp_075 tightening beta1→0.8 hurt +0.0013, so lengthening beta2 is the symmetric unexplored direction).

Replace per-row std with 1.4826×MAD (Median Absolute Deviation) in GPTQ scale computation — robust dispersion estimator gives finer quantization bins in the bulk for heavy-tailed LLM weight distributions while matching std exactly for Gaussian rows.

Zig-zag encode int8 Q tensors (v→((v<<1)^(v>>7)) as uint8) before torch.save to rescue exp_092's MAD-scale GPTQ (bpb=1.075144 but size_blocked) — lossless signed→unsigned transform converts 2's-complement bimodal byte layout to a unimodal-near-zero distribution that brotli-11's Huffman stage compresses tighter, freeing enough bytes to fit under cap.

Apply T=0.97 logit sharpening (divide pre-CE logits by 0.97) in eval_val_ttt scoring — mirror-direction test of exp_044's T=1.05 softening which was +0.002607 worse, indicating the post-GPTQ+TTT distribution is at or below calibration optimum; modest sharpening should pull val_bpb toward it.

Raise muon_wd 0.095→0.115 (+21%): direct sweep of the never-touched matrix-only weight-decay knob; tighter Linear-weight distributions should aid int6 GPTQ fidelity and add useful regularization pressure where prior forward-path noise (exp_040/050) and scalar-wd (exp_082/087) attempts failed.

Raise logit_softcap default 30.0→100.0 — effectively removes the tanh output squash for realistic logit magnitudes (|x|≲15 → <1% dampening vs ~4-8% at cap=30) while retaining asymptotic safety; loss-domain's one untouched model-side regularizer, in the direction consistent with the observed "remove regularization, not add" trend (z-loss hurt +0.003, adding 0.001 smoothing hurt +0.002).

Replace phase+k*seq_len packed start-indices with IID uniform random starts per sequence in ShuffledSequenceLoader._reset_shard: every sampled sequence now begins at an independent random offset, giving each training token a uniformly-random within-sequence context position instead of the packed scheme's single shared phase.
Apply fixed logit scale multiplier 3.0 to tied-embedding output projection
Add learnable per-channel gain to attn_norm and mlp_norm RMSNorms (LLaMA-style weighted RMSNorm) in every Block; final_norm left weightless since it's absorbable into the tied head.

NEFTune: inject uniform noise U(-α/√(T·D), α/√(T·D)) with α=5 on post-RMSNorm token embeddings during training only (disabled at TTT); a well-literature-validated regularizer not previously tried in this tree (exp_012 tried token replacement, which is structurally different).

Increase depth-recurrence num_loops 2→3: the shared loop segment [3..5] is replayed 4 times instead of 3, growing effective depth from 17 to 20 layers during active-recurrence phase — pure parameter-free capacity via the architecture's core param-golf trick.

Disable weight decay on scalar control tensors (adam_wd 0.02→0.0): q_gain=5.25, skip_weights, attn_scale, mlp_scale, resid_mix, skip_gates init at meaningful non-zero values — current AdamW coupled-WD compounds to ~80% shrinkage across the run (p *= (1-lr*wd)^steps), actively fighting the gradient. Standard practice (nanoGPT-speedrun, Llama) is no-WD on 1D params; untouched across the entire tree.
Revive orphaned exp_003 keep on current best lineage: EMA_DECAY 0.9965 to 0.998.

Restack exp_003's proven EMA_DECAY 0.9965→0.998 (-0.000776 keep) onto current best exp_177: the current chain (exp_000→005→010→088→…→177) never inherited exp_003's EMA widening since it branched through exp_005, so this reapplies a validated orthogonal averaging tweak atop the accumulated optimizer/loss/reg stack.

Swap Primer-style silu²(fc(x)) MLP for SwiGLU (silu(fc(x))*fc_up(x)), param-neutral via mlp_mult 4.0→2.625 (hidden 2048→1344, 3×512×1344=2.06M ≤ baseline 2×512×2048=2.10M per block, saves ~360K params).
Per-matrix Hessian-weighted scale refinement after GPTQ: closed-form solve for scalar α minimizing tr((W−αQ·diag(s)) H (W−αQ·diag(s))^T) and fold into per-row fp16 s (size-neutral).

Within-attention Muon WD split: QK-reader matrices (c_q, c_k) get muon_wd_qk=0.115 (21% boost) while V-reader + attn.proj stay at muon_wd=0.095; orthogonal to exp_164 (attn-vs-mlp type split) and exp_141 (recurrent depth split).
Add learnable per-vocab output logit bias to tied softmax. Decouples unigram-prior from embedding magnitude. exp_023 proposed but harness-aborted; this is a proper run.

Raise AdamW BETA1 0.9→0.92 on all AdamW groups (tied tok_emb + scalar params + optional lm_head): a smaller version of exp_136's 0.9→0.95 which size_blocked at bpb=1.077256 but never had its training signal confirmed, and a genuinely untested point between exp_075's failed 0.9→0.8 (discard +0.001267) and the LLM-standard 0.95.

Add 30% LR floor to TTT chunk-cosine schedule (cos_lr = ttt_lr · (0.3 + 0.7·cos_ratio)): late chunks (~last 15%) currently train at near-zero LR, leaving the val tail under-adapted; floor preserves peak LR at chunk 0 but keeps late chunks meaningfully learning for next-chunk scoring.

Intra-chunk linear LR warmup across TTT epochs: ep0 at 0.5*cos_lr → ep3 at 1.0*cos_lr (avg 0.75*cos_lr). Lets SGD momentum carried from previous chunk re-align with new chunk's gradient direction before the larger refinement steps.
Lower AdamW BETA2 0.95→0.93 on the tok_emb + scalar + head AdamW groups — untested direction after 4 prior raises all hurt (exp_068/091/159 β2=0.98, exp_126 β2=0.97).

Zero out adam_wd 0.005 to 0.0 on scalar AdamW group; continues exp_177's kept direction (0.02 to 0.005, -0.000038) to its endpoint. All params in this group are far from 0, so any WD is a misaligned pull-to-zero prior.

Apply T=0.98 sub-unity logit temperature in both eval_val_sliding and eval_val_ttt score passes (divide logits by 0.98 before cross_entropy): opposite-direction probe vs exp_044's T=1.05 failure (+0.00261), motivated by GPTQ-induced logit-noise softening of the post-softmax distribution.

Hard loss-mask on training positions 0..15 of each sequence (drop CE where context<16 tokens) to align training signal with the sliding-window eval metric (which scores ~98% of tokens at positions ≥1984).

Lower muon_momentum_warmup_start 0.92→0.90 (DOWN direction, mirror of discarded exp_015 UP): widens the warmup ramp span from 0.07 to 0.09 across the same 22% time-fraction, giving gentler momentum early when gradients are high-variance

Re-introduce exp_186's convex u^2 position-weighted CE (pw = 0.9 + 0.2*u^2, mean-preserved) onto current best — the kept improvement was inadvertently dropped by exp_190's forward() refactor and has been missing from the best chain since.
Halve GPTQ multiplicative Hessian damping 1.01→1.005 (scalar-downward continuation of the monotonic winning trajectory exp_280 1.02→exp_377 1.01).
Apply mean-preserving linear position-weighted CE (linspace 0.9→1.1) to the TTT inner-SGD loss so gradient upweights long-context positions — the positions that actually score at stride=64.
Lower GPTQ multiplicative Hessian damping 1.01 → 1.005 (−50% damping strength): continuation of the kept DOWN trajectory 1.02 (exp_280 keep) → 1.01 (exp_377 keep).

Add z-loss (lambda=1e-5, logsumexp^2) to main training forward — same formula as exp_596's proven TTT z-loss, applied to main training CE to regularize logit magnitudes during training; final BPB metric unaffected (eval_val_sliding/TTT use forward_logits directly).
